title: hieracium nigrescens subsp. scoliodon
intro: Hieracium nigrescens subsp. scoliodon, commonly known as Hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The therapeutic actions of Hawkweed are attributed to its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Hawkweed include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Hieracium nigrescens subsp. scoliodon can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its potential toxicity. In severe cases, improper use of this plant can lead to liver damage and kidney failure. Additionally, there is a risk of allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and difficulty breathing. If ingested in large quantities or over an extended period, Hieracium nigrescens subsp. scoliodon can cause more severe symptoms, such as seizures and coma.
